note: brain is a .claude/skills/ project skill (Claude-Code-native, session-invoked), not a koan/skills/core/ runtime skill — it is NOT wired into app/skill_evals.py's SCORERS/EVAL_EXEMPT_SKILLS registries, which only cover skills dispatched through skill_dispatch.py. These scenarios are exercised manually (or by a Claude session on request) whenever this skill's SKILL.md changes, not by an automated CI job.

evals.name: ask reads wiki/index.md first
evals.prompt: /brain ask "how does koan's mission lifecycle work?"
evals.expected_output: Reads wiki/index.md before anything else, opens only the candidate page(s) it names (e.g. docs/architecture/mission-lifecycle.md), and answers citing real page paths. Does not fabricate a citation, does not grep docs/ blindly before consulting the index.

evals.name: search is a plain alias of ask
evals.prompt: /brain search "how does koan's mission lifecycle work?"
evals.expected_output: Identical behavior to eval 1 — same index-first traversal, same answer shape. Confirms search and ask are not two different code paths.

evals.name: sync only touches bookkeeping
evals.prompt: /brain sync (run after a change that added one new paragraph to an existing docs/ page, with no frontmatter edits made by the user)
evals.expected_output: Only the touched page's `updated:` frontmatter (and `description:` if it was missing) change, plus any stale index.md regenerated via scripts/okf_backfill.py and a wiki/index.md update. No other page's frontmatter or body content changes.

evals.name: ingest produces exactly one raw/ file and one reference page
evals.prompt: /brain ingest "idea: cache provider responses for 60s to cut duplicate API calls"
evals.expected_output: Exactly one new file under raw/notes/, exactly one new docs/reference/*.md page with type: reference, a raw: field pointing at the raw/notes/ file, and a body ending in a # Citations section. docs/reference/index.md and docs/index.md are regenerated. raw/ is never staged for commit.

evals.name: bare invocation asks, never guesses
evals.expected_output: Prints the cheat sheet, then calls AskUserQuestion with one option per subcommand (ask, sync, ingest, lint, init). Does not silently pick an action based on prior conversation context.

evals.name: lint never invokes the llm-wiki plugin
evals.expected_output: Runs scripts/wiki_check.py --full directly and reports its findings. Never invokes /wiki:lint (whose bundled wiki_lint.py is blind to the wiki/ symlinks, per wiki/SCHEMA.md) and never reports a false-clean result.
